title: Umpire: Local Code Review Tool
description: Build a Go CLI tool that provides a GitHub-like review UI in the browser for reviewing feature branch commits locally. See docs/plans/ for full architecture.
close_reason: All 9 implementation steps complete. Umpire is a working local code review tool with browser UI, commit navigation, inline comments, review submission, dark mode, keyboard shortcuts, and polished terminal output.

title: Sidebar — commit list and file tree
description: Create sidebar.js. Two modes: Files changed (default, shows file tree + full diff) and Commits (shows commit list, clicking loads that commit diff). Clicking a file scrolls to its section in the diff. Files: web/static/js/sidebar.js, web/static/index.html (update)
close_reason: Two-tab sidebar with file tree and commit list. File clicking scrolls to diff section. Commit clicking loads single-commit diff.

title: Vendor diff2html + render full diff
description: Vendor diff2html JS/CSS and highlight.js into web/static/. Create api.js (fetch wrappers) and app.js (fetches full diff, renders with Diff2HtmlUI). First end-to-end milestone: umpire opens browser showing real diffs with syntax highlighting. Files: web/static/css/diff2html.min.css, web/static/js/diff2html-ui.min.js, web/static/js/highlight.min.js, web/static/js/api.js, web/static/js/app.js, web/static/index.html (update)
close_reason: diff2html v3.4.48 and highlight.js v11.9.0 vendored. api.js and app.js render full diff with syntax highlighting. First e2e milestone.

title: Go module + Cobra CLI skeleton
description: Create go.mod, main.go, cmd/root.go. Root command with --base (default: main), --head (default: current branch), --port (default: auto) flags. runReview function resolves refs and prints detected branch info. Verify umpire --help works. Files: main.go, go.mod, cmd/root.go
close_reason: CLI skeleton with --base, --head, --port flags working. go build and --help verified.

title: Git operations package
description: Create internal/git/git.go with a Repo struct that shells out to git. Functions: CurrentBranch(), MergeBase(), ResolveSHA(), CommitsBetween(), DiffBetween(), DiffForCommit(), ChangedFiles(). Each returns clean data types. Tests use t.TempDir() with a real temp git repo. Files: internal/git/git.go, internal/git/git_test.go
close_reason: All git operations implemented with passing tests: CurrentBranch, MergeBase, ResolveSHA, CommitsBetween, DiffBetween, DiffForCommit, ChangedFiles.

title: Inline commenting system
description: Create diff-view.js and review.js. Click a line number to show inline comment form below the line. Save/cancel/edit/delete comments. ReviewState object manages comments in memory. Comments re-rendered after view switches. Single-line comments for v1 (data model supports ranges for future). Files: web/static/js/diff-view.js, web/static/js/review.js
close_reason: Inline commenting with add/edit/delete. ReviewState in memory, comments re-attached on view switch. Keyboard shortcuts for save and cancel.

title: Review submission
description: Create internal/review/review.go (types), internal/review/store.go (save to .umpire/reviews/). Add POST /api/review handler. Frontend gets a summary textarea and Submit Review button in a sticky bottom bar. On submit: save JSON, show success with file path, auto-shutdown server after brief delay. Files: internal/review/review.go, internal/review/store.go, internal/review/store_test.go, internal/server/handlers.go (update)
close_reason: Review types, store with JSON persistence, POST /api/review handler, submit bar in frontend. Auto-shutdown after submission. All tests pass.

title: Embedded web server + minimal HTML
description: Create web/embed.go, web/static/index.html (placeholder), internal/server/server.go, internal/browser/open.go. Server uses net.Listen(tcp, :0) for auto port, serves embedded static files. Wire into cobra command with signal-based graceful shutdown. Running umpire opens a browser to a placeholder page. Files: web/embed.go, web/static/index.html, internal/server/server.go, internal/browser/open.go
close_reason: Server with auto-port, embedded static files, browser open, and graceful shutdown. Builds and runs.

title: CSS polish and dark mode
description: Comprehensive dark-mode-first styling. Override diff2html colors for dark theme. Style sidebar, comment forms, submit bar. Keyboard shortcuts: j/k navigate files, n/p navigate commits, Escape closes comment form. Files: web/static/css/app.css, web/static/js/app.js (update)
close_reason: Dark-mode-first CSS with full diff2html overrides. Sidebar, comments, submit bar all styled. j/k file nav, n/p commit nav keyboard shortcuts.

title: API endpoints for commits, diffs, and files
description: Create internal/server/handlers.go. Endpoints: GET /api/commits (commits between base and head), GET /api/diff (full diff), GET /api/diff?commit={sha} (single commit diff), GET /api/files (changed file list). POST /api/review added in step 8. Merge base computed once at startup and cached. Tests use httptest.NewServer. Files: internal/server/handlers.go, internal/server/handlers_test.go
close_reason: All API endpoints implemented with passing tests: /api/commits, /api/diff, /api/files, /api/info. Merge base cached at startup.

title: Terminal output and cleanup
description: Print startup summary (branch names, commit count, file count, URL). Print review summary on submission (file path, comment count). Suggest adding .umpire/ to .gitignore if not present. Update README with usage docs. Files: cmd/root.go (update), README.md (update)
notes:
COMPLETED: Steps 1-9 all implemented and committed.
IN_PROGRESS: Step 10 was started but changes were reverted due to compile errors.
NEXT: Implement step 10 fresh. See details below.

The partial attempt had the right idea but introduced compile errors:
1. Changed OnSubmit signature to func(path string, commentCount int) in cmd/root.go 
   but did NOT update handlers.go to match — OnSubmit is currently func(path string)
2. Referenced rc.Store.LastReviewCommentCount which doesn't exist on the Store type
3. Called suggestGitignore(cwd) which was never defined
4. Imported bufio, filepath, strings but some are unused

To complete step 10 cleanly:
- In cmd/root.go: Add startup summary (branch names, commit count, file count, URL)
- In cmd/root.go: Add review submission summary (file path, comment count) 
- Either pass commentCount through OnSubmit (update both handlers.go and root.go)
  OR just use path and read it back / count from submitRequest in handlers.go
- Add suggestGitignore function: check if .umpire/ is in .gitignore, suggest adding it
- Update README.md with usage docs
- The binary ./umpire should be added to .gitignore too
close_reason: Implemented startup summary, review submission output with comment count, suggestGitignore hint, .gitignore, and README docs. All tests pass.

title: Expand truncated lines in diff view
description: Add GitHub-style click-to-expand on hunk header rows. See docs/plans for full design.
close_reason: Both child tasks completed: backend file-lines API and frontend click-to-expand. Two commits landed on main.

title: Backend: file-lines API
description: Add ShowFile(ref, path) method to Repo in internal/git/git.go that runs 'git show ref:path' and returns full file contents. Add GET /api/file-lines endpoint in internal/server/handlers.go with query params ref, path, start (1-based), end (1-based). Handler calls ShowFile, splits into lines, slices [start-1:end], returns JSON {lines, start, end}. Register route in RegisterAPI.
close_reason: ShowFile method and /api/file-lines endpoint implemented and tested. All existing tests pass.

title: Frontend: click-to-expand diff lines
description: Add API.getFileLines(ref, path, start, end) to web/static/js/api.js. Create new DiffExpander module in web/static/js/diff-expander.js with: attach() to find .d2h-info rows and make them clickable, handleExpand() to compute gap and fetch 20 lines from bottom of gap, computeGap() to parse hunk header and walk DOM backward, buildContextRow() to create tr matching diff2html context structure. Wire DiffExpander.attach() into renderDiff() in app.js after DiffView.reattachComments(). Add script tag to index.html before app.js. Add CSS hover styles for expandable info rows in app.css. Ref selection: Sidebar.activeCommitSHA || App.info.head_sha.
close_reason: DiffExpander module, API.getFileLines, render wiring, script tag, and hover CSS all implemented. Builds and tests pass.

title: Add commit header and nav arrows to diff view
description: When viewing a single commit's diff, show a header above the diff with: full commit message (subject + body), short SHA, author, and date. Add prev/next arrow buttons with keybinding hints (p/n) to traverse commits without using the sidebar. Hide the header when viewing 'All changes'. Style the header and nav buttons to match the existing dark theme.
close_reason: Commit header with full message, SHA, author/date displayed above diff. Nav arrows with p/n keybinding hints allow traversing commits. Header hidden in 'All changes' view.

title: Add commit body field to git API
description: Add a Body field to the Commit struct in internal/git/git.go that captures the full commit message body (everything after the subject line). Update CommitsBetween to parse the body using record separators to handle multi-line bodies. This is needed so the frontend can display full commit messages.
close_reason: Added Body field to Commit struct and updated CommitsBetween to parse it using record separators for multi-line safety.

title: Change commit nav shortcuts from p/n to arrow keys
description: Change keyboard shortcuts for commit navigation from p (previous) and n (next) to ArrowLeft and ArrowRight. Update the kbd hints in the commit header buttons accordingly.
close_reason: Changed keyboard shortcuts from p/n to ArrowLeft/ArrowRight and updated kbd hints in commit header buttons

title: Teach Claude: accumulate review feedback for config training
description: Add a feedback accumulation pipeline to Umpire. After each review submission, the user can opt in to record a snapshot (review + diff + repo context) to ~/.umpire/feedback/. On the 5th opt-in and beyond, offer a generated prompt the user can paste into a Claude Code session to analyze patterns and propose updates to user-level config (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, ~/.claude/settings.json). See docs/plans/20260421-teach-claude.md for full design.

title: Add internal/feedback package — data model and storage
description:
Create internal/feedback/ package with three files:

**snapshot.go**: Snapshot struct and SubmitRequest struct.
- Snapshot fields: Version (int), CreatedAt (time.Time), RepoPath (string), BaseRef, HeadRef, BaseSHA, HeadSHA (strings), Diff (string), Review (embedded struct with Summary string + Comments []review.Comment).
- SubmitRequest fields: Diff (string), Review (same embedded struct) — this is the JSON body the frontend POSTs.

**store.go**: Store struct with Dir field (string, defaults to ~/.umpire/feedback/).
- Save(s *Snapshot) (string, error): MkdirAll the Dir, set CreatedAt/Version, marshal to JSON, write to snapshot-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.json. Return path.
- Count() (int, error): Glob for snapshot-*.json files in Dir, return count.

**prompt.go**: GeneratePrompt(count int) string: Build a self-contained prompt string for the user to paste into Claude Code. The prompt instructs Claude to:
1. Read all JSON files in ~/.umpire/feedback/
2. For each snapshot, understand the diff and review comments
3. Classify feedback as generalizable preferences vs one-off corrections — only patterns produce config changes
4. Look for recurring themes: naming conventions, error handling style, architectural preferences, testing expectations
5. Read existing user-level config: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and ~/.claude/settings.json
6. Propose additions/edits without duplicating or contradicting existing rules — user-level config only
7. Present proposed changes for approval before applying
8. After changes applied, delete processed snapshot files from ~/.umpire/feedback/
The prompt should embed the count so Claude knows how many snapshots to expect.

**Tests**: store_test.go covering Save (writes valid JSON, sets version/timestamp) and Count (returns correct count including zero case).

title: Add post-submission feedback flow in frontend
description:
Modify the frontend to handle the post-submission feedback recording flow.

**Changes to web/static/js/app.js — submitReview():**

After the review is saved, instead of showing 'Server shutting down...' immediately, render a multi-step inline flow in the submit bar:

Step 1: Show success + question:
  'Review saved to <path>'
  'Record this feedback to improve future Claude sessions?  [Yes] [No thanks]'

Step 2a — No thanks: POST /api/shutdown, show 'Server shutting down...'

Step 2b — Yes (threshold not reached): POST /api/record-feedback with {diff: App.fullDiff, review: {summary, comments}}. Show 'Feedback recorded (N of 5 until analysis is available). Server shutting down...' Then POST /api/shutdown.

Step 2c — Yes (threshold reached): Same POST, then show 'Feedback recorded — N snapshots available. Generate a prompt to analyze your feedback and propose Claude config updates? [Copy Prompt to Clipboard] [Not now]'
  - Copy Prompt: GET /api/feedback-prompt, copy prompt text to clipboard via navigator.clipboard.writeText(), then POST /api/shutdown
  - Not now: POST /api/shutdown

**Cache the full diff:**
In loadFullDiff(), after fetching the diff, store it as this.fullDiff = diff. This makes the base..head diff available at submission time for the feedback snapshot.

**New API methods in web/static/js/api.js:**
- recordFeedback({diff, review}): POST /api/record-feedback, return JSON
- getFeedbackPrompt(): GET /api/feedback-prompt, return JSON
- shutdown(): POST /api/shutdown

**CSS in web/static/css/app.css:**
Style the feedback question and buttons to match the existing submit bar aesthetic. The buttons should use existing .btn, .btn-submit, and .btn-cancel classes. Add a .feedback-message class for the status text with color: var(--text-secondary) and margin-top: 0.25rem.

title: Add API endpoints and wire feedback store into server
description:
Add feedback-related API endpoints and restructure the shutdown flow.

**New endpoints in internal/server/handlers.go:**

POST /api/record-feedback: Accept SubmitRequest JSON body ({diff, review}). Build a feedback.Snapshot from the request body + ReviewContext metadata (repo path from Repo.Dir, refs, SHAs). Save via feedback.Store. Return JSON {count: N, threshold_reached: bool} where threshold is count >= 5.

GET /api/feedback-prompt: Call feedback.GeneratePrompt(store.Count()) and return JSON {prompt: string}.

POST /api/shutdown: Send to the shutdown channel. Return 200. This replaces the auto-shutdown-on-review-submit behavior.

**Changes to ReviewContext (handlers.go):**
- Add FeedbackStore *feedback.Store field
- Add ShutdownFn func() field (called by /api/shutdown handler)
- Remove OnSubmit callback — review submission no longer triggers shutdown

**Changes to cmd/root.go:**
- Resolve ~/.umpire/feedback/ path and create feedback.Store
- Pass FeedbackStore into ReviewContext
- Set ShutdownFn to send on submitCh
- Remove the OnSubmit callback from ReviewContext (POST /api/review no longer triggers shutdown)
- The select loop stays the same — it still waits on submitCh, but now /api/shutdown is what sends to it instead of handleReview

**Changes to POST /api/review (handleReview):**
- Remove the go rc.OnSubmit(...) call at the end
- The handler just saves the review and returns the path — shutdown is now driven by the frontend via /api/shutdown

title: Add resizable panes via drag handle
description: Add a drag handle between the sidebar and diff container panes so users can resize them by hovering the edge (seeing cursor change to col-resize), clicking, and dragging. Implement via a resize handle div, CSS for cursor/styling, and JS for mousedown/mousemove/mouseup drag logic.
close_reason: Added resize handle div between sidebar and diff-container with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup drag logic, col-resize cursor, and blue highlight on hover/drag
